label: SES-066 PI-026 historical-applies-as-deposit-events backfill planned: 5 decisions settled (DEC-206 references included in wrote_record edges, DEC-207 sequential identifier allocation Option A, DEC-208 reference resolution forward-then-reverse-then-skip with data-quality exception logging, DEC-209 apply_context conventions for backfilled DEPs, DEC-210 24-record scope after orphan-CONV constraint excluded SES-001 and SES-046); 24 close_out_payload records (COP-009..COP-032) and 24 deposit_event records (DEP-020..DEP-043) with 221 wrote_record back-edges specified for out-of-band landing via the PI-026 backfill prompt. 1 session, 5 decisions (DEC-206..210), 5 references (5 decided_in).

session.title: PI-026 historical-applies-as-deposit-events backfill planned: 24 close_out_payload records (COP-009..COP-032) and 24 deposit_event records (DEP-020..DEP-043) specified for ses_012 through ses_053 historical close-outs, with 221 wrote_record back-edges including reference targets per Option 2; SES-001 and SES-046 deferred alongside DEC-197's orphan-session pattern due to absent CONV parents required by close_out_payload.md §3.4.5
session.conversation_reference: Claude.ai ARCHITECTURE-mode planning conversation. Opened against the PI-026 kickoff at PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/pi-026-historical-applies-deposit-events-backfill-kickoff.md to scope and settle the metadata for 24 close_out_payload and 24 deposit_event records covering the historical close-out payloads from ses_012 through ses_053. The conversation rebased the kickoff's anticipated DEP starting identifier: kickoff said DEP-016; SES-063 and SES-064 had applied via the normal real-time path since the kickoff was authored, consuming DEP-016 and DEP-017; the SES-066 close-out apply itself will consume DEP-018 (apply_close_out.py auto-creates one deposit_event per apply); PI-026's allocation therefore starts at DEP-020. The conversation surfaced and resolved one consequential decision the kickoff did not flag (whether to include references as wrote_record targets given Phase 1's deliberate skip) and confirmed the kickoff-flagged identifier-allocation question (Option A continues sequential). Three data-quality findings emerged during inventory authoring: ses_001's three references stored as session->decision in the snapshot vs decision->session in the payload (direction-inverted resolution required); ses_030's four references unresolvable in either direction because DEC-105/106/107 are decided_in SES-036, not SES-030, in the snapshot (ses_030/ses_036 appear to be a duplicate-session artifact from an early re-application); and the close_out_payload.md §3.4.5 producing-conversation edge required-at-all-statuses rule excluded SES-001 and SES-046 from PI-026's scope because their CONV parents do not exist (both are in DEC-197's orphan-session list).
session.topics_covered:
Seed prompt: "Project default operating mode: ARCHITECTURE.\n\nUse PROTOTYPE mode when explicitly declared in a kickoff prompt for early exploratory work..." attached to the conversation's opening turn alongside the PI-026 kickoff path (PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/pi-026-historical-applies-deposit-events-backfill-kickoff.md).

Pre-flight verification. Pulled both crmbuilder and CBM repos, read both CLAUDE.md files, read PI-026 kickoff plus PI-025 kickoff for predecessor context, skimmed Phase 1 and PI-025 backfill scripts as precedent, read close_out_payload and deposit_event schema specs for required fields and edge-rule constraints. Confirmed current identifier heads against the CRMBUILDER engagement's db-export snapshot: sessions SES-064, decisions DEC-204, planning items PI-045, close-out payloads COP-064, deposit events DEP-017. Confirmed SES-063 and SES-064 had applied via the real-time path (DEP-016 and DEP-017 consumed) — the kickoff's pre-flight gate cleared. Confirmed the 26-JSON inventory matches the kickoff exactly (ses_001, ses_012..015, ses_025..027, ses_029..044, ses_046, ses_053).

Identifier rebase. The kickoff anticipated DEP-016 as the first PI-026-allocated DEP identifier. Real-time apply path consumed DEP-016 and DEP-017 between kickoff authoring and this conversation's open (SES-063 and SES-064 close-out applies). During this conversation's authoring window, PI-045's planning conversation closed and committed its SES-065 close-out payload to origin at 9a465a0 — that apply, when run, will consume DEP-018. This conversation's SES-066 close-out apply will consume DEP-019. Under the conversation's Option-A identifier allocation, PI-026's DEP range therefore becomes DEP-020 through DEP-045 pre-orphan-shrink and DEP-020 through DEP-043 after the orphan-CONV constraint shrink described below.

Decision 1 — references in wrote_record edges (DEC-206). The kickoff's literal scope language enumerated "session / decisions / planning_items / references" as the four target-type sets for wrote_record back-edges. Phase 1's backfill_governance_phase_1.py deliberately skipped references with the inline comment "skip references — REF-NNNN lookup adds complexity for limited value during backfill" and set records_summary.references=0 on DEP-001..008 to satisfy the spec's count-equals-edge-count cross-check. PI-026 settled this in favor of the kickoff's literal scope (Option 2): the backfill script's reference resolver maps each payload reference entry to its REF-NNNN identifier and authors a wrote_record edge per resolvable reference. Outcome differs from Phase 1's precedent by 125 reference edges (the resolvable subset of 139 payload references across the 24-record post-shrink inventory). Phase 1's 8 historical references remain orphan from the apply-event graph due to deposit_event immutability (records_summary cannot be amended via PATCH); documented in in_flight_at_end.

Decision 2 — identifier allocation (DEC-207). Kickoff-flagged surface-and-confirm question with kickoff-default Option A. Option A continues the sequential allocation pattern Phase 1 used (COP-001..008 are sequential, not session-matched); PI-026's COPs allocate COP-009..COP-032 and DEPs allocate DEP-020..DEP-043 in inventory order. Option B (match-to-SES for backfilled records) would have required either schema-level identifier-format change to admit a backfill suffix or an offset numbering range, plus retroactive renumbering of Phase 1's records (blocked by deposit_event immutability for the DEP side). Option A approved; the resulting convention reads "backfilled COPs/DEPs are sequential; real-time records match the session they apply to."

Decision 3 — reference resolution strategy (DEC-208). Three data-quality patterns surfaced during inventory authoring: (a) 132 payload references resolve directly via forward-direction match in the references snapshot; (b) 3 payload references in ses_001.json resolve only via reverse-direction match — the payload stores them as decision->session decided_in while the snapshot stores them as session->decision decided_in (REF-0001/0002/0003, the first three references ever created in the database); (c) 4 payload references in ses_030.json are unresolvable in either direction because DEC-105/106/107 are decided_in SES-036 in the snapshot rather than SES-030 (ses_030 and ses_036 share the same title and appear to be duplicate-session artifacts of an early re-application). Settled: backfill script's reference resolver tries forward match first, then reverse match, then skips with a logged data-quality exception. Skipped references reduce both the wrote_record edge count and the records_summary.references field for the affected DEP by the matching count.

Decision 4 — apply_context conventions (DEC-209). Kickoff-flagged surface-and-settle questions resolved per kickoff defaults. apply_script_version="backfill" (the actual version that ran the historical apply is not recorded; the runner field carries the provenance). invocation synthesized as "backfill_pi_026: ../PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/close-out-payloads/ses_NNN.json" per close-out JSON. runner="backfill_script". log_file_path follows Phase 1's dep_NNN-historical.log pattern with three-line content noting the apply ran pre-v0.7 and was backfilled by scripts/backfill_pi_026_historical_applies_deposit_events.py on the apply date.

Decision 5 — 24-record scope (DEC-210). close_out_payload.md §3.4.5 requires every COP to have exactly one outbound close_out_payload_produced_by_conversation edge at all statuses, enforced at POST time with HTTP 422 payload_requires_producing_conversation_edge if the edge is absent. The kickoff's 26-JSON inventory included SES-001 and SES-046; neither session has a corresponding CONV record (both are in DEC-197's orphan-session list — SES-001..010 predate systematic workstream tracking; SES-046 is the WS-001-establishing conversation Phase 1 explicitly left orphan). PI-026 cannot author COP-records for those two sessions without first backfilling their CONV records, and CONV records themselves require workstream parents which are not yet established for the pre-workstream-era. Settled: defer SES-001 and SES-046 close-outs alongside DEC-197's existing orphan pattern. PI-026's scope shrinks from 26 to 24 close-outs; COP range becomes COP-009..COP-032 and DEP range becomes DEP-020..DEP-043; total wrote_record edges becomes 221 (24 sessions + 63 decisions + 9 planning items + 125 references).

Inventory deliverable approved. The 24-row per-COP allocation table mapping each historical close-out JSON to its allocated COP identifier, allocated DEP identifier, CONV parent identifier, and parsed record counts was presented as a block and approved before the artifact-authoring step. The data structure feeds directly into the backfill script's _CLOSE_OUTS list.
session.artifacts_produced: PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/close-out-payloads/ses_066.json (this payload — bundles 1 session record, 5 decisions DEC-206..210, 5 decided_in references); PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/prompts/CLAUDE-CODE-PROMPT-apply-close-out-ses-066.md (apply prompt for this payload); PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/prompts/CLAUDE-CODE-PROMPT-pi-026-historical-applies-deposit-events-backfill.md (installs and runs crmbuilder-v2/scripts/backfill_pi_026_historical_applies_deposit_events.py — lands 24 close_out_payload records COP-009..COP-032, 24 deposit_event records DEP-020..DEP-043, 24 placeholder log files dep_020-historical.log..dep_043-historical.log, and 269 reference edges total: 24 close_out_payload_produced_by_conversation, 24 deposit_event_applies_close_out_payload, and 221 deposit_event_wrote_record (24 session + 63 decision + 9 planning_item + 125 reference targets)).
session.in_flight_at_end:
PI-026 backfill prompt queued for execution. Apply order: this close-out (SES-066) first to land DEC-206..210 plus the supporting decided_in references; then the backfill prompt to land COP-009..COP-032, DEP-020..DEP-043, and the 269 supporting reference edges. PI-026 closes the Phase 4 portion of the PI-022 governance-backfill program; PI-023 (workstream-state reconciliation utility) follows and closes the PI-022 program.

Two close-out payloads on disk deferred to a future orphan-conversation backfill: ses_001.json and ses_046.json. Both sessions are in DEC-197's orphan-session list and have no CONV parent records; close_out_payload.md §3.4.5 requires the producing-conversation edge at all statuses, so no COP can be authored until those CONV records exist. Backfilling those CONVs in turn requires workstream parents that do not exist for the pre-workstream era. A future tight follow-up addresses the broader orphan-conversation backfill question for the 16 DEC-197 orphan sessions; that follow-up's planning item is not yet allocated.

Phase 1's references-orphan finding noted explicitly: DEP-001..008 carry records_summary.references=0 by Phase 1 design (Phase 1 deliberately skipped references-as-wrote_record-targets per its inline comment). PI-026's DEC-206 departs from that precedent, but DEP-001..008 cannot be retroactively amended to include reference wrote_record edges or updated records_summary.references counts under deposit_event's born-terminal append-only lifecycle (POST + GET only; no PUT, PATCH, or DELETE; records_summary cross-check enforces summary-count equals edge-count at write time). The asymmetry — Phase 1 historical apply graph has reference-orphan DEPs, PI-026 historical apply graph has full reference coverage — is permanent under the current spec.

ses_030 / ses_036 duplicate-session artifact flagged as future cleanup candidate. ses_030.json and ses_036.json share the same session title ("Styling Conversation 2 — build planning: ui-PRD-v0.6..."), both reference DEC-105/106/107, and the database has both SES-030 and SES-036 as separate session records. The decided_in edges in the snapshot point to SES-036; the wrote_record edges PI-026 authors will attribute the 3 decisions to DEP-028 (for ses_030's apply) based on creation-time evidence (decisions were created at 03:23 on 05-18-26, after SES-030 was created at 02:33 but before SES-036 was created at 04:31). The 4 references ses_030.json claims it created are unresolvable; DEP-028's records_summary.references=0 and zero wrote_record edges for references. Future cleanup may merge SES-030 and SES-036 as duplicates or formally retire one; PI-026 takes no position and authors records as the data currently stands.

Next conversation: PI-023 (workstream-state reconciliation utility — reads the populated workstream / conversation / session / close_out_payload / deposit_event graph after PI-024 / 025 / 026 land and verifies internal consistency). Kickoff at PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/pi-023-workstream-state-reconciliation-utility-kickoff.md (to be authored at PI-026's close per the kickoff chain rule). PI-023 closes the PI-022 governance-backfill program.

decisions.title: PI-026 includes references as deposit_event_wrote_record targets, departing from Phase 1's precedent of skipping them
decisions.context: The PI-026 kickoff's scope language enumerated four target-type sets for the wrote_record back-edges: "session / decisions / planning_items / references". Phase 1's backfill_governance_phase_1.py deliberately skipped references with the inline comment "skip references — REF-NNNN lookup adds complexity for limited value during backfill" and set records_summary.references=0 on DEP-001..008 to satisfy the spec's count-equals-edge-count cross-check. The kickoff did not flag this precedent gap as a surface-and-settle question; the conversation discovered it during preflight when comparing Phase 1's actual script behavior to the kickoff's literal scope. The decision determines whether PI-026's audit-event graph is complete for references or matches Phase 1's lossy pattern.
decisions.decision: PI-026 authors deposit_event_wrote_record edges for resolvable references in each historical close-out payload, in addition to the wrote_record edges for sessions, decisions, and planning_items. The backfill script's reference resolver maps each payload reference entry to its REF-NNNN identifier via the references snapshot, attempting forward-direction match first then reverse-direction match, and authors one wrote_record edge per resolvable reference. records_summary.references on each DEP carries the count of resolvable references (matching the count of wrote_record edges authored, per the spec cross-check).
decisions.rationale: The kickoff's literal scope language is the authoritative spec for PI-026. The reference-lookup complexity Phase 1 named as the rationale for its skip is straightforward to discharge: a single pass over the references snapshot builds a lookup table keyed by (source_type, source_id, target_type, target_id, relationship_kind) → REF-NNNN, and resolution becomes O(1) per payload entry. The audit-graph completeness gain is real: after PI-026 lands, the query "which apply created REF-NNNN?" returns the responsible DEP-NNN for 125 of 139 payload references (the 14 unresolvable references are documented as data-quality exceptions in DEC-208's resolution rules and in the in_flight_at_end notes). Phase 1's 8 historical references remain orphan because deposit_event records are immutable; the precedent asymmetry is acknowledged as permanent under the current spec.
decisions.alternatives_considered: Match-Phase-1-precedent — skip references as wrote_record targets. Rejected because the kickoff's literal scope calls for inclusion, the lookup complexity is one-time-trivial, and the audit-graph completeness payoff is permanent. Sub-question — backfill the 8 Phase 1 historical references' wrote_record edges retroactively. Rejected because deposit_event is born-terminal append-only (POST + GET only); records_summary on DEP-001..008 is frozen at references=0 and cannot be amended via PATCH; adding wrote_record edges without amending records_summary would silently break the spec's cross-check invariant.
decisions.consequences: PI-026's backfill script gains a reference-resolution stage with forward-then-reverse-then-skip semantics (DEC-208 details). Each DEP record's records_summary.references field carries the resolvable-reference count rather than 0. Total wrote_record edges PI-026 authors increases from 105 (sessions + decisions + planning_items only) to 221 (with references included). The future apply path (apply_close_out.py running real-time) already writes wrote_record edges for references; PI-026's choice aligns the historical-era graph with current behavior for all sessions PI-026 covers. Phase 1's references-orphan pattern stands as a permanent historical asymmetry.

decisions.title: PI-026 continues sequential identifier allocation for backfilled COP and DEP records (Option A), preserving Phase 1's pattern
decisions.context: The kickoff explicitly flagged identifier allocation as a surface-and-confirm question with two options. Option A continues the sequential numbering Phase 1 used: COP-001..008 are sequential by allocation order, not matched to the session each applies to. Option B would have used match-to-SES numbering for backfilled records, requiring either a schema-level identifier-format change to admit a backfill suffix (e.g., COP-012-h) or an offset numbering range (e.g., COP-101..). The kickoff default was Option A. The decision locks in the convention for any future historical-backfill work.
decisions.decision: PI-026 allocates COP-009 through COP-032 (24 records) and DEP-020 through DEP-043 (24 records) sequentially by inventory order against the historical close-out JSONs. The convention reads going forward: "Backfilled COPs and DEPs are sequential by allocation order; real-time records match the session they apply to." Phase 1's COP-001..008 follow the same sequential pattern; real-time records COP-056..064 and DEP-009..017 follow the match-to-SES pattern.
decisions.rationale: Matches Phase 1's existing pattern, preserves the schema's ^COP-\d{3}$ and ^DEP-\d{3}$ identifier regexes with no change, avoids retroactive renumbering (which is blocked by deposit_event immutability for the DEP side regardless), and avoids inventing an offset numbering convention that has no precedent. The numbering inconsistency between backfilled (sequential) and real-time (match-to-SES) is factually accurate to the project history.
decisions.alternatives_considered: Option B — match-to-SES for backfilled records. Two sub-variants considered: (i) admit a -h suffix to the identifier regex (schema change touching the ^COP-\d{3}$ / ^DEP-\d{3}$ constraints, the access-layer validation, and Alembic migrations); (ii) use an offset like COP-101..126 / DEP-101..126 to encode "historical of session NNN" implicitly. Either way required leaving Phase 1's COP-001..008 as the only sequential-numbered exception or retroactively renumbering them (blocked by deposit_event immutability for DEP-001..008). Rejected on schema-stability and minimum-viable-change grounds; the kickoff's flagged default carried.
decisions.consequences: PI-026's allocation is locked: COP-009..COP-032 to ses_012, ses_013, ses_014, ses_015, ses_025, ses_026, ses_027, ses_029, ses_030, ses_031..044, ses_046 [excluded — see DEC-210], ses_053 in inventory order; DEP-020..DEP-043 in matching positions. Audit queries that ask "which COP covered SES-NNN?" require a refs/file_path lookup rather than an identifier inspection — acceptable since both the COP and DEP UIs are read-only audit logs where lookup is the normal access pattern. Future historical-backfill work (e.g., the deferred orphan-conversation backfill that would land SES-001 and SES-046's COPs) inherits the same sequential pattern from the next available identifier.

decisions.title: Reference-resolution strategy for historical close-out payloads: forward-then-reverse-then-skip, with skip events logged as data-quality exceptions
decisions.context: DEC-206's resolution requires the backfill script to map each historical close-out payload's reference entries to their corresponding REF-NNNN identifiers in the references snapshot, so the script can author deposit_event_wrote_record edges pointing at those REFs. During inventory authoring, three patterns emerged: (a) most payload references resolve directly when matched by (source_type, source_id, target_type, target_id, relationship_kind); (b) three payload references in ses_001.json store decision->session decided_in while the snapshot stores them as session->decision decided_in — same conceptual edge, inverse direction; (c) four payload references in ses_030.json don't resolve in either direction because the relevant snapshot edges target SES-036 rather than SES-030. The script needs a deterministic policy for each pattern.
decisions.decision: The backfill script's reference resolver applies three steps in order, stopping at the first success: (1) Forward match — look up (source_type, source_id, target_type, target_id, relationship_kind) in the references snapshot; if found, use the corresponding REF-NNNN as the wrote_record edge target. (2) Reverse match — swap source and target and look up again; if found, use that REF-NNNN. Reverse matches are logged with a note distinguishing them from forward matches. (3) Skip — log a data-quality exception with the payload file, the unresolvable reference entry, and the message "reference unresolvable in either direction; skipped"; do not author a wrote_record edge for this entry; do not fail the script. records_summary.references for the affected DEP is decremented to match the actual count of wrote_record edges authored.
decisions.rationale: Three-tier resolution captures the actually-observed data patterns without inventing semantics the snapshot doesn't support. Forward match handles the typical case (132 of 139 payload references). Reverse match handles the direction-inversion case (3 references in ses_001.json — the earliest references created in the database, stored by an apply path that wrote decided_in in canonical session->decision direction while the close-out payload format later adopted the decision->session direction). Skip handles cases where no edge exists in either direction (4 references in ses_030.json — the close-out's reference list describes edges that were never actually created in the database in the form the payload claims). Logging skips as data-quality exceptions preserves auditability without forcing the script to abort; the exceptions are documented in SES-066's in_flight_at_end.
decisions.alternatives_considered: Strict forward-only resolution — would have failed PI-026 by requiring abort on the 3 ses_001 reverse-direction references; rejected. Synthesize-missing-REFs strategy — create new reference records to match what the payload claims; rejected because (a) the references already exist in alternate direction or alternate session for the two affected patterns, (b) creating duplicates would corrupt the snapshot, (c) the payload's claims about ses_030 are factually inconsistent with database state. Per-file-special-case resolution — hardcode the 7 exceptions in the script; rejected as fragile to future similar patterns and as encoding data-quality issues opaquely. The three-tier policy generalizes to any future historical-payload backfill encountering similar issues.
decisions.consequences: PI-026 authors 125 reference wrote_record edges in the post-shrink scope. Decomposition: 132 forward-resolvable across all 26 historical close-outs minus 7 forward-resolvable in ses_046 (deferred by DEC-210) = 125 forward-resolvable in scope; plus 3 reverse-resolvable in ses_001 (also deferred by DEC-210) = 0 reverse-resolvable in scope; plus 4 unresolvable in ses_030 that remain in scope and are skipped per DEC-208 contributing 0 wrote_record edges. DEP-028 (for ses_030) carries records_summary.references=0 and zero outbound wrote_record edges for references; all other DEPs PI-026 authors have records_summary.references matching their resolved-reference counts. Skip events log to the script's stdout (and the script names the four skipped references in its console output); the four ses_030 skips are also explicitly named in SES-066's in_flight_at_end.

decisions.title: Apply_context and log_file_path conventions for PI-026 backfilled deposit_event records
decisions.context: The deposit_event spec section 3.2.6 requires three JSON fields populated on every record: records_summary, apply_context, and (conditionally) error_info. apply_context's required keys are apply_script_version, invocation, and runner. The deposit_event_log_file_path field is also required (non-empty, repo-relative, unique within the engagement). For real-time applies, apply_close_out.py populates these fields automatically. For backfilled applies, the historical apply's actual invocation, script version, and stdout log are not recorded. The kickoff flagged these as surface-and-settle questions with proposed defaults; the conversation confirmed the defaults and locks them in as the PI-026 convention.
decisions.decision: PI-026's backfill script populates deposit_event records with: (1) apply_context.apply_script_version = "backfill" — the version that ran the original historical apply is not recorded; the runner field carries the provenance, version doesn't add information. (2) apply_context.invocation = "backfill_pi_026: ../PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/close-out-payloads/ses_NNN.json" per close-out JSON. Synthesizes a representative string that names the script and the payload file path. (3) apply_context.runner = "backfill_script". (4) deposit_event_log_file_path follows Phase 1's pattern: "PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/deposit-event-logs/dep_NNN-historical.log" where NNN matches the DEP identifier. Each log file contains three lines: a header naming the DEP, a line noting "This apply ran pre-v0.7 (before deposit_event logging existed).", and a backfill provenance line naming the backfill script and timestamp.
decisions.rationale: Each convention follows Phase 1's precedent or the kickoff's default. apply_script_version="backfill" is semantically more precise than picking an arbitrary historical version string. The synthesized invocation string is unique per record (file path varies), human-readable, and self-identifying as backfilled rather than real-time. runner="backfill_script" matches Phase 1 verbatim. The log_file_path convention parallels Phase 1's dep_NNN-historical.log naming, preserves uniqueness (each DEP gets its own log file), and the three-line content matches Phase 1's content shape; the placeholder log file is committed alongside the deposit_event POST per the spec's file-pointer semantics (the path is repo-relative, not validated for existence at write time, but the file is provided for completeness).
decisions.alternatives_considered: Real apply_script_version — attempt to identify the actual version that was running at the historical apply moment by git-log on the close-out file's commit timestamp. Rejected as low-value; the runner field already captures the backfill provenance; spurious precision would imply a level of forensic recoverability the records don't actually support. Use script's invocation command verbatim — "python scripts/backfill_pi_026_historical_applies_deposit_events.py" as the invocation string for all 24 DEPs. Rejected because it loses per-record specificity (every DEP carries the same string); the per-JSON synthesized invocation preserves which payload was applied. Sparse log file content (empty file) — rejected because deposit_event_log_file_path's spec semantics admit an empty file but Phase 1's three-line placeholder provides enough context for a forensic reader to identify the file as a backfill placeholder without consulting external state.
decisions.consequences: PI-026 authors 24 deposit_event records with the four conventions above, and writes 24 placeholder log files (dep_018-historical.log through dep_042-historical.log) committed alongside the regenerated db-export snapshot. The conventions are documented for any future historical-backfill work that follows the same pattern (e.g., the deferred orphan-conversation backfill, when it lands).

decisions.title: PI-026 scope shrinks to 24 close-outs after the close_out_payload producing-conversation edge constraint excludes SES-001 and SES-046
decisions.context: close_out_payload.md §3.4.5 establishes the production-edge-required-at-all-statuses rule: every close_out_payload record must have exactly one outbound close_out_payload_produced_by_conversation edge regardless of status (including drafted), enforced at the access layer with HTTP 422 payload_requires_producing_conversation_edge if the edge is absent on POST. The edge cannot be added via PATCH later — the record cannot exist without it. PI-026's original 26-JSON inventory included ses_001.json and ses_046.json. Neither SES-001 nor SES-046 has a corresponding conversation record: both are explicitly in DEC-197's orphan-session list (SES-001..010 "predate systematic workstream tracking"; SES-046 is the WS-001-establishing conversation Phase 1 explicitly left orphan). Backfilling their CONV records in turn requires workstream parents that do not exist for the pre-workstream era. PI-026 must either shrink its scope or expand to include CONV backfill (and workstream creation) for the pre-workstream era.
decisions.decision: PI-026's scope shrinks from 26 to 24 close-outs. SES-001's close-out (ses_001.json) and SES-046's close-out (ses_046.json) are deferred alongside DEC-197's existing orphan-session pattern. COP range adjusts to COP-009..COP-032 (24 records); DEP range adjusts to DEP-020..DEP-043 (24 records). Total wrote_record edges decrease to 221 (24 sessions + 63 decisions + 9 planning items + 125 references). The deferred close-outs and their backfill requirements are documented in SES-066's in_flight_at_end as a future tight follow-up that addresses the broader orphan-conversation backfill question for the 16 DEC-197 orphan sessions.
decisions.rationale: Aligns with DEC-197's deferral logic — those sessions are already in the orphan list for related reasons, and the close_out_payload spec's producing-conversation requirement is a hard constraint at the access layer. Expanding PI-026's scope to backfill the missing CONV records would expand the scope to include backfilling a pre-workstream-era workstream record (or some other parent shape for those CONVs), which the conversation declined to invent on this conversation's facts. Deferral preserves PI-026's tight planning-item shape (one focused phase of the PI-022 program) and consolidates the broader orphan-conversation question for a future tight follow-up.
decisions.alternatives_considered: Expand PI-026 — backfill the 2 missing CONV records inline. Requires creating workstream parents for them (the pre-workstream era has no workstream record; SES-046 is in WS-001's domain but Phase 1 explicitly excluded it). Rejected on scope-creep grounds. Skip the producing-conversation edge for these two COPs — would require either (a) spec amendment to make the edge optional for historical-backfill cases (a substantive cross-spec change rejected on architectural-purity grounds) or (b) hacking the access layer (rejected outright). The deferral path preserves the spec invariants and produces a clean PI-026 deliverable.
decisions.consequences: PI-026 authors 24 COPs and 24 DEPs rather than 26. SES-001 and SES-046's close-out JSONs remain on disk but unreferenced by any COP record; both are documented as future-work candidates in SES-066's in_flight_at_end. The full DEC-197 orphan-session list (16 sessions) remains as a future tight follow-up whose planning item is not yet allocated. PI-023 (the workstream-state reconciliation utility that closes the PI-022 program) will report on these orphan close-outs alongside any other invariant violations it surfaces.
